reasoning: Keir Starmer became UK Prime Minister in July 2024 following a massive landslide general election victory. With a parliamentary working majority of over 170 seats, the likelihood of an internal party coup, losing a vote of no confidence, or calling an early election in his first full calendar year in office (2025) is extremely low. Historically, a newly elected UK Prime Minister with a large majority is highly secure for at least their first few years. Thus, the only realistic scenarios for him vacating the office in 2025 involve sudden severe health issues, death, or an unprecedented scandal. These background risks combine for an estimated probability of around 5%.
